issues.description: Explicit self-assignment during stall ('id_pc <= id_pc', 'id_instr <= id_instr', 'id_valid <= id_valid') is unnecessary. Registers inherently hold their values when not loaded, so these statements cause unnecessary toggling and do not affect functionality.
issues.fix_suggestion: Remove the 'else if (stall)' branch entirely, or replace the self-assignments with comments explaining the stall behavior. The default behavior of a register will maintain the values when stalled.

summary: The if_id_reg module is well-designed and correctly implements a pipeline register with proper synchronous reset (active low), flush, and stall functionality. All outputs are properly registered and the priority order is correct (reset > flush > stall > normal). Port declarations are correct with appropriate widths. The explicit self-assignments during stall are functionally correct but unnecessary and could be optimized for cleaner code.
